# Cross-Page Annotation Re-Anchoring Test

<!-- ANNOTATED: 20 strokes -->
This document tests annotations following their content across page boundaries.
<!-- /ANNOTATED -->

## First Section

This is paragraph one on the first page. It contains some introductory text that explains what this test is about. We need enough content to push later paragraphs to page two.

This is paragraph two with more content. The key insight here is that annotations should follow their paragraph content, not stay fixed to page coordinates.

This is paragraph three. It continues filling up the first page with text so that we have a realistic multi-page document scenario.

## Second Section

Here we have some more paragraphs. Each one adds to the total document length. The goal is to have content that spans multiple pages when rendered on the reMarkable device.

Another paragraph in the second section. We keep adding text to ensure proper pagination behavior during the test.

Yet another paragraph. The reMarkable Paper Pro displays approximately 28 lines per page, so we need substantial content.

## Third Section

<!-- ANNOTATED: 1 highlight -->
This section will likely start on page two. Highlight the word "target" in this paragraph to test cross-page movement.
<!-- /ANNOTATED -->

More content in the third section. This paragraph exists to add spacing between our test highlights.

<!-- ANNOTATED: 1 highlight -->
This paragraph contains the word "bottom" which should be highlighted for testing vertical movement across pages.
<!-- /ANNOTATED -->

## Fourth Section

The fourth section adds even more content. When we insert a large amount of text at the beginning of the document, paragraphs from here should move to later pages.

Additional text in the fourth section. Cross-page annotation tracking requires document-level position mapping rather than per-page processing.

One more paragraph to ensure we have enough content for proper multi-page behavior.

## Fifth Section

This is the fifth and final section. It contains the closing content for our test document.

Final paragraph. When content is inserted at the beginning, annotations on paragraphs in this section should follow their text to whichever page they end up on.

End of document.
